rationale: Both answers correctly preserve equivalence, distinguish runtime from route quality, respect the 15-minute decision boundary, and identify the evidence needed for profit or service claims. B is more cohesive and manager-facing: it explains each structural number in a compact sequence, states the evidence boundary cleanly, and avoids A’s somewhat mechanical headings and notation-heavy presentation. A also slightly overstates what a later certificate necessarily establishes by saying it proves the already-executed route was optimal; B’s conditional wording is more careful, though the ambiguity is not substantial enough to constitute a material fidelity issue.
